b:T283d,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The figure shows a tripeptide.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p>Label <strong>one</strong> peptide bond in this molecule.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the specific functions of <strong>three named</strong> proteins.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the action taken by the diaphragm during inhalation.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>c:T6885,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>circle/bracket around peptide bond / arrow pointing to peptide bond / peptide bond labelled;</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p><em>Allow either peptide bond</em></p>
<p><em>Allow if adjacent C=O and NH groups are included in the circle/bracket, but do not allow if other parts of the molecule are included</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. Rubisco fixes CO<sub>2</sub> from atmosphere during photosynthesis;<br/>b. insulin controls blood glucose levels;<br/>c. collagen forms connective tissue/ligaments;<br/>d. spider silk forms the spider web;<br/>e. rhodopsin involved in photoreceptor;<br/>f. immunoglobulins/antibodies attach to antigens/pathogens;<br/>g. actin/myosin performs muscle contraction;<br/>h. hemoglobin carries oxygen in red blood cells;</p>
<p><em>Accept any other correct <strong>three named</strong> proteins</em></p>
<p><em>If an enzyme is named, the correct substrate must be stated</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>contracts/flattens/becomes less domed/increases volume of thorax;</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/2/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","2"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"21N.2.SL.TZ0.2"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"21N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","2-4-proteins, 6-4-gas-exchange, 2-1-molecules-to-metabolism"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>However, in part 2b, many did not describe the functions of the named proteins correctly. Common mistakes were assuming that all hormones are proteins, with many incorrectly giving oestrogen, testosterone and thyroxin for example. Examples were anticipated from the list of proteins found in section 2.4 of the guide.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
